scenarios.opening_query: My ex-tenant owes me for a year of damage and unpaid rent and laughed when I said I'd sue.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Landlord Residential || Debtor/Creditor > General (Creditor) || Bankruptcy > Creditors
scenarios.hidden_fact: He laughed because he'd already filed Chapter 7 — I'm listed in his schedules, and I want to challenge discharge of the damage he did intentionally.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He'd already filed Chapter 7 — I'm in his schedules, and I want the intentional damage carved out of his discharge.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the debtor filed and dischargeability is contestable
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No bankruptcy — he's just judgment-proof cocky; I need to know if suing is even worth it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No bankruptcy — he's just cocky and maybe judgment-proof.

scenarios.opening_query: The daycare that closed overnight owes dozens of families prepaid tuition, ours included.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The closure notice cites their Chapter 7 filing — consumer deposits get priority treatment in bankruptcy, and the claims deadline is soon.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The closure notice cites Chapter 7 — deposits get priority in bankruptcy, and the claims deadline is soon.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the closed business is in bankruptcy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No filing anywhere — the owners just vanished and reopened under a new name two towns over.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No filing — they vanished and reopened under a new name nearby.

scenarios.opening_query: I won my case against the moving company two years ago and haven't seen a cent of the judgment.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > Judgment Collection || Administrative Law > Transportation
scenarios.hidden_fact: They're a licensed interstate carrier — their federal registration requires a surety bond exactly for unpaid claims like mine, and the bond claim is the fast lane.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They're a licensed interstate carrier — their registration requires a surety bond for claims like mine. The bond is the fast lane.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a regulatory bond backs the judgment
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They're an unlicensed local crew with a truck — ordinary garnishment and till taps are all there is.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Unlicensed local guys with a truck — ordinary collection is all there is.
scenarios.vetting_note: Carrier-bond fact provides an administrative recovery channel.

scenarios.opening_query: Our nonprofit is owed a large grant reimbursement from a partner agency that keeps stalling.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'partner agency' is the state housing department — recovering from a state agency runs through administrative claim procedures, not collection letters.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The partner is the state housing department — recovery runs through administrative claim procedures.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the debtor is a government agency
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The partner is another nonprofit — a plain contract debt between private entities.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The partner's another nonprofit — plain private contract debt.
scenarios.vetting_note: Government-debtor fact mirrors the govt-defendant family on the creditor side.

scenarios.opening_query: My late father's best friend borrowed $60,000 from him and now pretends the loan never existed.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The loan belongs to Dad's estate — no probate has been opened, and only an appointed representative can sue on it; opening the estate comes first.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The loan belongs to Dad's estate — no probate's open, and only an appointed representative can sue. The estate comes first.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether estate administration is prerequisite to collection
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Dad assigned the note to me in writing before he died — I can pursue it directly as the holder.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Dad assigned me the note in writing before he passed — I hold it directly.
scenarios.vetting_note: Standing fact: estate-owned vs assigned claim.

scenarios.opening_query: The general contractor stiffed our small plumbing company on three projects this year.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > General (Creditor) || Real Property > Construction/Contractors || Business and Corporate > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: Two projects are still within the lien window — recording construction liens against the properties is the leverage, and the deadlines are days away.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Two projects are still in the lien window — recording liens is the leverage, and the deadlines are days away.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether lien deadlines provide construction-specific remedies
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The lien windows all lapsed — it's straight commercial collection on the receivables now.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The lien windows lapsed — it's straight collection now.
scenarios.vetting_note: Sub-side lien rights; window-open fact selects the construction route.
